projectDescription: A production-grade, modular Snakemake pipeline for end-to-end chromatin accessibility analysis supporting both bulk and single-cell ATAC-seq, with upfront configuration validation, strict QC gating, and execution profiles spanning laptops to Kubernetes clusters.
keyFeatures: Dual-modality support for bulk and single-cell ATAC-seq from a single config file
Upfront configuration validation with categorized error reporting before DAG construction
Four-metric QC gate (FRiP, TSS enrichment, mapping rate, duplicate rate) with graceful fallbacks
Eight pre-built execution profiles spanning local workstations to AWS, GCP, Azure, and Kubernetes
Full Tn5 bias-corrected TF footprinting via TOBIAS BINDetect and HINT-ATAC
IDR replicate concordance, consensus peak calling, and DESeq2 differential accessibility
scATAC-seq: Chromap alignment, ArchR Arrow files, doublet detection, UMAP clustering, Cicero co-accessibility
GEOAgent bridge for automated conversion of GEO metadata to ready-to-run pipeline config
LangChain tool wrapper exposing the pipeline as an autonomous AI agent tool
Machine-readable pipeline execution summary JSON with per-rule CPU and memory benchmarks
Docker container with micromamba runner; individual rule environments via Conda/Singularity
Sequential batching script for ultra-low-memory execution on ≤4 GB RAM machines
publicApiSurface: run_atacseq_pipeline(profile, cores, use_conda, batch_size, config_override_path, geo_metadata_csv, download_geo, project_dir) [LangChain @tool]
docker build -t bdb-atacseq . && docker run -it --rm -v $(pwd):/app bdb-atacseq --use-conda --cores 8

config.yaml keys: global, fastp, fastqc, bowtie2, samtools_*, tn5_shift, picard, tss_enrichment, fragment_size_analysis, qc_gate, macs2, blacklist_filter, frip_calculation, idr, consensus_peaks, differential_accessibility, tobias, footprinting, chromvar_analysis, bigwig, heatmap, multiqc, chromap, archr, cicero
